title: Effect of Acetylsalicylic Acid and Sildenafil on the Longevity of Spruce Branches: A Triple-Blinded Randomized Control Trial
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"yliai","text":"Christmas trees are engaged in a race against time, from being cut down to Christmas Eve, where their beauty must be maximized. An old housewife's remedy recommends adding an aspirin tablet to the water for the Christmas tree to extend its period of looking beautiful (Aesthetic Lifespan: AL). However, there is no evidence supporting this advice, so we pose the question: Can acetylsalicylic acid (ASA) prolong the lifespan of Norway spruces in terms of AL? We will include 60 spruce branches divided into three groups of 20 and examine the effects of ASA compared to a placebo group, as well as a group where we intervene with Sildenafil. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gvynp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ovfci","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ek0f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zg3q3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkna6","text":"Our hypothesis is that the antipyretic effect of aspirin will lower the internal temperature of the spruce branches, bringing them closer to the conditions in which the tree was cut down - the cold December weather.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgsrl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"qw7fd","text":"Context and intent of the study: While the research question may appear whimsical, it is essential to emphasize that the methodology employed in the investigation is rigorous and serious. Unconventional studies can still contribute valuable insights to the scientific community, especially in the context of the Christmas edition of several medical journals, where a touch of lightheartedness is often welcomed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aduji","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lfalm","text":"As for the hypothesis, it is based on a “household tip”, that Aspirin kan prolong the lifespan of Christmas trees. We propose (as a possible explanation to the old saying) that the antipyretic effect of acetylsalicylic acid might lower the internal temperature of the spruce branches, thereby simulating conditions closer to the environment in which the tree was cut down. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdqzo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gycux","text":"The rationale behind this hypothesis stems from the well-established understanding that aspirin can lower fever in humans by affecting certain biochemical pathways. Therefore, we hypothesize that a similar effect might be observed in plant tissues, specifically spruce branches, as they too experience physiological responses akin to fever when exposed to stressors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ygfk3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"udcjf","text":"The inclusion of sildenafil as a third intervention group in our study is based on intriguing observations in other biological tissues. Sildenafil's vasodilatory properties have been associated with prolonging certain biological lifespans, maintaining rigidity and functionality.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8gnto","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4k9v3","text":"The term \"Triple-Blinded Randomized Control Trial\" refers to a study design where not only the participants but also the investigators and outcome assessors are blinded to the treatment groups. This method aims to reduce bias and ensure the credibility of the study's findings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vifv3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"rlgw2","text":"Medication 1: Acetylsalicylic acid, 75 mg/l","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"baikf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xbwcn","text":"Medication 2: Acetylsalicylic acid 750 mg/l","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zlc7j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oyb97","text":"Medication 3: Sildenafil 25 mg/l","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"khvpy","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gtgdd","text":"Medication 4: Sildenafil 250 mg/l","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rituw","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14pot","text":"Medication: Placebo","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7iyo4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4kkgw","text":"Procedure: Dosage solution in a water glass","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ntilj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the aspirin, sildenafil, and placebo treatments according to appropriate dosages and administration methods. Ensure that the placebo closely resembles the active treatments in appearance. 4 treatment solutions are prepared: (1) 150 mg aspirin/5 L of water, (2) 1500 mg aspirin/5 L of water, (3) 50 mg sildenafil/5 L of water, (4) 50 mg sildenafil/5 L of water. The solution preparation is done by a separate researcher.

Allocate 60 identical glass containers to specific solutions, ensuring that 10 containers are assigned to each treatment allocation and 20 containers to the placebo. This task must be carried out by an independent researcher.
Place a spruce branch in each glass, make sure to collect predefined variables before administration of therapy.

Regularly monitor the spruce branches in all groups throughout the study period. Assess and record the relevant measurements of lifespan criteria at predetermined intervals (e.g., daily). This should be completed by independent researchers.
Use standardized methods and tools for data collection to ensure consistency and accuracy.

After the study period is complete, collect all the data and perform appropriate statistical analysis. Use methods such as ANOVA to compare three groups, logistic regression to assess survival within 24 days and Cox survival analysis (analysis of variance) to compare the lifespan measurements among the three groups.
Through our research, we posit that branches treated with aspirin will exhibit a prolonged Aesthetic Lifespan (AL) compared to their counterparts receiving different treatments.
